evidence_quotes.quote: companies are able to shut down a game (digital service) without advance notice or even explaining their reasons why.
Leaving it in a playable state so gamers can take action themselves when a company decides not to work on a game anymore should be a base right.

paragraphs.text: As an avid gamer, I would kindly request that ownership of games is also taken into consideration. As it stands nowadays, companies are able to shut down a game (digital service) without advance notice or even explaining their reasons why. Leaving it in a playable state so gamers can take action themselves when a company decides not to work on a game anymore should be a base right. A good example and probably with a better and more extensive explanation can be found in the 'stop killing games' initiative. Thanks in advance!
